classes.title: Beginner Evening Yoga
classes.subtitle: Gentle intro to yoga basics
classes.description: A calm, beginner-friendly yoga class focusing on breathing, stretching, and simple poses. Perfect for those new to yoga or returning after a break.
classes.requirements: Comfortable clothing; yoga mats available to borrow.

classes.title: Beginner Yoga Foundations
classes.subtitle: Early evening stretch
classes.description: Learn foundational yoga postures with extra attention to alignment and safety. Ideal for first-timers.
classes.requirements: Bring a water bottle; mats provided.

classes.title: Morning Beginner Yoga
classes.subtitle: Start your day with movement
classes.description: An energizing morning yoga sequence with simple flows and short guided relaxation.

classes.title: Intermediate Vinyasa Yoga
classes.subtitle: Flow and strength
classes.description: A moderately paced vinyasa flow class with creative sequencing and core work. Some prior yoga experience recommended.
classes.requirements: Comfortable clothing; bring your own mat if preferred.

classes.title: Advanced Power Yoga
classes.subtitle: Intense, fast-paced flow
classes.description: Challenging sequences, arm balances, and inversions for experienced practitioners.
classes.requirements: At least 1 year of regular yoga practice.

classes.title: Zumba Dance Party
classes.subtitle: High-energy cardio dance
classes.description: Dance-based cardio with Latin and global music. No dance experience required.

classes.title: Family Music & Movement
classes.subtitle: Kids and caregivers together
classes.description: Sing-along songs, rhythm games, and simple dance moves for young children and their adults.
classes.requirements: Best for ages 1–6 with an adult.

classes.title: Mat Pilates Basics
classes.subtitle: Core strength and posture
classes.description: Introductory Pilates class focusing on alignment and core stabilization.
classes.requirements: Mats provided; socks or bare feet.

classes.title: Watercolor Evenings
classes.subtitle: Relaxed painting group
classes.description: Explore watercolor techniques in a friendly, non-judgmental setting. Supplies included for first-time attendees.
classes.requirements: Wear clothes you don't mind getting paint on.

classes.title: Morning Swim Fitness
classes.subtitle: Laps and drills
classes.description: Structured swim sets focusing on endurance and technique. Lane assignments by ability.

classes.title: Community Board Game Night
classes.subtitle: Drop-in social play
classes.description: Bring your favorite games or try ours. Light instruction for new players and a rotating featured game each week.
classes.requirements: Open to ages 12+; under 16 must attend with an adult.

classes.title: Chair Yoga for Seniors
classes.subtitle: Gentle movement with support
classes.description: Low-impact seated and standing exercises using a chair for balance, designed for older adults and those with limited mobility.

classes.title: Beginner Yoga Sampler
classes.subtitle: Past session recording
classes.description: A one-time introductory class that has already taken place. Included here for schedule history.

classes.title: Evening Spin Class
classes.subtitle: High-intensity cycling
classes.description: Indoor cycling class with interval training and motivating music.

classes.title: Intro to Ceramics
classes.subtitle: Hand-building basics
classes.description: Learn simple hand-building techniques to create small bowls and cups. Includes glazing in a future session.

clubs.name: Evening Book Discussion Circle
clubs.description: A relaxed group that meets weekly to discuss contemporary fiction, memoirs, and the occasional classic.
clubs.participation_guidelines: Read at least half of the selected book before each meeting; respectful discussion is expected.

clubs.name: Weekday Nature Walks Group
clubs.description: Short evening walks exploring local parks and river trails, suitable for most fitness levels.
clubs.participation_guidelines: Wear comfortable walking shoes; minors must attend with an adult.

clubs.name: Board Games & Strategy Night
clubs.description: A social group for fans of modern board games, card games, and light strategy titles.
clubs.participation_guidelines: Be kind to new players; allow different games to rotate; ages 14+.

clubs.name: Saturday Hiking Club
clubs.description: Longer weekend hikes in nearby state parks and nature reserves.
clubs.participation_guidelines: Sign up for each hike in advance; bring water, snacks, and appropriate footwear.

clubs.name: Poetry & Spoken Word Open Mic
clubs.description: Share original poetry or favorite readings in a cozy, supportive setting.
clubs.meeting_recurrence: First Friday of each month at 7:30 PM
clubs.participation_guidelines: Limit readings to 5 minutes; content must remain respectful and community-appropriate.

clubs.name: Afternoon Chess Club
clubs.description: Casual and competitive chess play with informal lessons for beginners.
clubs.participation_guidelines: All ages welcome; younger players should be supervised by an adult.

clubs.name: Family Crafters Circle
clubs.description: Simple crafts and DIY projects suitable for kids and adults.
clubs.participation_guidelines: Children must attend with an adult; materials fee may apply for some projects.

clubs.name: Language Exchange Meetup
clubs.description: Practice English, Spanish, and other languages in an informal conversation setting.
clubs.participation_guidelines: Be patient with language learners; no sales or solicitations.

clubs.name: Sunday Film Buffs Club
clubs.description: Watch and discuss independent, international, and classic films.

clubs.name: Makerspace Open Lab
clubs.description: Use 3D printers, basic electronics tools, and craft supplies with guidance from volunteers.
clubs.participation_guidelines: Safety orientation required on first visit; follow posted tool rules.

forum_boards.name: General Discussion
forum_boards.description: Talk about anything related to the club and community life.

forum_boards.name: Sports & Fitness
forum_boards.description: Share tips, find workout buddies, and discuss sports leagues and classes.

forum_boards.name: Arts & Culture
forum_boards.description: Discuss arts events, classes, performances, and creative projects.

forum_boards.name: Family & Kids Activities
forum_boards.description: Connect with other families and share ideas for kid-friendly fun.

membership_plans.name: Basic Gym
membership_plans.description: Affordable access to the fitness center during staffed hours.
membership_plans.access_privileges: Access to cardio and weight equipment during standard operating hours.
membership_plans.conditions: Month-to-month; cancel anytime with 30 days notice.

membership_plans.name: Evening Saver Combo
membership_plans.description: Discounted evening-only membership with both gym and pool access.
membership_plans.access_privileges: Access to gym and lap pool after 3:00 PM on weekdays and all day on weekends.
membership_plans.conditions: Month-to-month; enrollment fee waived during seasonal promotions.

membership_plans.name: Active Combo
membership_plans.description: Full-day access to gym and pool with member discounts on classes.
membership_plans.access_privileges: Unlimited access to gym and pool during all open hours; 10% off paid classes.

membership_plans.name: Premium All-Access
membership_plans.description: Our most comprehensive plan with extra guest passes and facility perks.
membership_plans.access_privileges: Unlimited access to all fitness and aquatics areas, plus monthly guest passes and early registration for popular programs.
membership_plans.conditions: 12-month agreement; guest pass usage subject to capacity limits.

membership_plans.name: Pool Plus Family
membership_plans.description: Family-focused plan with pool access and limited gym usage.
membership_plans.access_privileges: Access to lap and leisure pools for up to 4 household members.
membership_plans.conditions: Plan currently unavailable to new sign-ups; existing members may renew.

news_articles.title: January Open Mic Night Brings New Voices to the Stage
news_articles.excerpt: Local performers filled the Community Hall with music and poetry during our first open mic of the year.
news_articles.content: Our January Open Mic Night showcased 18 performers ranging from first-time singers to seasoned spoken-word artists. The event drew over 70 audience members, many of whom stayed afterward to chat and collaborate on future performances. We plan to continue hosting open mics on the first Friday of each month, with signups opening two weeks in advance.

news_articles.title: Spring Community Concert Preview: Ensembles You Won’t Want to Miss
news_articles.excerpt: From jazz combos to youth choirs, this year’s spring concert is our most diverse yet.
news_articles.content: The annual Spring Community Concert will feature five ensembles, including the Club Jazz Collective, the Intergenerational Choir, and a new acoustic singer-songwriter circle. Rehearsals are already underway, and there are still a few open spots in the choir for altos and tenors. The concert is scheduled for April 19, and tickets will be pay-what-you-can with a suggested donation of $10.

news_articles.title: Local Band Spotlight: Meet Riverstones, Our New House Band
news_articles.excerpt: Riverstones blends folk, rock, and blues in a sound that’s quickly becoming a club favorite.
news_articles.content: Riverstones began as a trio meeting in our practice rooms and has grown into a five-piece band that now performs regularly at club events. Their sets feature original songs inspired by river life and local history, along with creative covers of classics. The band will headline our upcoming Summer Kickoff Party and will also host a monthly songwriting workshop open to all levels.

news_articles.title: Summer Jam Session Wrap-Up
news_articles.excerpt: Musicians from across town joined for an improvised evening of music-making.
news_articles.content: Our July Jam Session brought together over 20 musicians spanning ages 14 to 72. The event highlighted the club’s growing music community and tested new sound equipment in Community Hall A.

news_articles.title: Family Spring Fair Returns with New Activities
news_articles.excerpt: Carnival games, craft booths, and live music will fill the courtyard at this year’s fair.
news_articles.content: The Family Spring Fair will take place on April 5, featuring expanded craft booths, community organization tables, and a dedicated toddler play zone. Volunteers are needed for setup, games, and cleanup shifts.

news_articles.title: Winter Gala Raises Funds for Youth Programs
news_articles.excerpt: Thanks to our members and sponsors, the gala exceeded its fundraising goal.
news_articles.content: This year’s Winter Gala raised over $12,000 for youth scholarships and program supplies. Highlights included performances by the youth dance troupe and a silent auction featuring local artwork.

news_articles.title: Updated Pool Hours and Lap Lane Schedule
news_articles.excerpt: Starting March 15, the pool will open earlier on weekdays and add dedicated family swim times.
news_articles.content: To meet growing demand, weekday pool opening times will shift from 7:00 AM to 6:30 AM, and additional evening lap lanes will be added. Family swim sessions will now run on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 4:00–6:00 PM.

news_articles.title: Membership Rate Adjustments Effective July 1
news_articles.excerpt: A modest rate increase will support facility upgrades and new programs.
news_articles.content: Beginning July 1, monthly membership rates will increase by an average of 4%. This change will fund new cardio equipment, expanded youth programming, and lighting upgrades throughout the building.

news_articles.title: New Pickup Basketball Nights Added to Schedule
news_articles.excerpt: Casual full-court games now available on Tuesday and Thursday evenings.
news_articles.content: Members have requested more open gym time for casual basketball. In response, we’ve added pickup basketball sessions on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 7:30–9:30 PM. All skill levels are welcome.

news_articles.title: Running Group Adds New Evening Route
news_articles.excerpt: A new 5K loop along the river will be tested this month.
news_articles.content: Our running group will pilot a new 5K evening route along the riverfront trail, starting next Wednesday at 6:15 PM. Runners of all paces are encouraged to attend, and reflective gear is recommended.

news_articles.title: Volunteer of the Month: Celebrating Alex Rivera
news_articles.excerpt: Alex has contributed over 50 hours to family events and facility projects this year.
news_articles.content: From coordinating check-in at the Family Fall Fest to helping repaint the Kids Activity Room, Alex has become a familiar and friendly face at many events. We’re grateful for their ongoing support and leadership.

news_articles.title: Family Game Night Draws Record Crowd
news_articles.excerpt: Over 40 families attended the latest Family Game Night, filling the club with laughter and friendly competition.
news_articles.content: Families rotated through tabletop games, cooperative challenges, and a life-sized board game in the gym. Based on feedback, we’ll be adding a dedicated quiet games room at future events for those who prefer a calmer environment.

news_articles.title: Club Launches Sustainability Initiative
news_articles.excerpt: New recycling stations and energy-saving upgrades are coming this spring.
news_articles.content: The club is rolling out a multi-phase sustainability plan that includes new recycling and composting bins, LED lighting upgrades, and incentives for members who arrive by bike or public transit.

news_articles.title: Songwriting Circle to Launch This Summer
news_articles.excerpt: A new group will support aspiring songwriters with prompts and feedback.
news_articles.content: This upcoming program will pair beginning songwriters with experienced mentors in a small-group setting. Details are still being finalized and will be announced in a future article.

news_articles.title: Jazz Night Series Comes to a Close
news_articles.excerpt: After three successful seasons, the monthly jazz series is taking a hiatus.
news_articles.content: Our long-running jazz series has featured dozens of local musicians and guest artists. While the program is pausing, we hope to bring it back in a new format in the future.

rooms.name: Community Hall A
rooms.description: Large multi-purpose hall suitable for performances, parties, and big meetings.

rooms.name: Conference Room 2
rooms.description: Medium meeting room with flexible seating and built-in projector.

rooms.name: Game Room 1
rooms.description: Casual space with large tables ideal for board games and small gatherings.

rooms.name: Yoga & Mindfulness Studio
rooms.description: Quiet studio with mirrored wall and soft lighting, ideal for yoga and meditation.

rooms.name: Kids Activity Room
rooms.description: Colorful room set up for crafts, games, and youth programs.

rooms.name: Conference Suite B
rooms.description: Flexible meeting suite that can be configured as one large room or divided into two smaller spaces.

workshops.title: Latin Dance Crash Course
workshops.description: Learn the basics of salsa and bachata in a fun, social evening workshop. No partner required.
workshops.prerequisites: None; wear comfortable shoes that allow you to turn easily.

workshops.title: Evening Watercolor Workshop
workshops.description: Experiment with washes, layering, and simple landscapes in this guided painting session. All supplies included.
workshops.prerequisites: Recommended for ages 15+; no painting experience necessary.

workshops.title: Intro to Acoustic Guitar
workshops.description: Learn basic chords, strumming patterns, and how to accompany simple songs.
workshops.prerequisites: Bring your own acoustic guitar; limited loaners available by request.

workshops.title: Smartphone Photography Walk
workshops.description: Improve your smartphone photography while exploring the neighborhood at golden hour.
workshops.prerequisites: Charge your phone fully and clear some storage space for new photos.

workshops.title: Saturday Pottery Intensive
workshops.description: A three-hour crash course in wheel throwing and glazing, with pieces fired for later pickup.
workshops.prerequisites: Wear clothes you don't mind getting clay on; short nails recommended.

workshops.title: Kids Coding Lab: Game Design
workshops.description: Kids learn basic coding concepts by designing simple games using block-based programming tools.

workshops.title: Financial Wellness 101
workshops.description: Learn the basics of budgeting, saving, and setting realistic financial goals.
workshops.prerequisites: Open to adults 18+; bring a notebook or device for notes.

workshops.title: Family Music Jam Workshop
workshops.description: Parents and kids explore rhythm instruments and simple songs together.
workshops.prerequisites: Best for ages 3–8 with an adult.

workshops.title: Winter Mindfulness Retreat (Past)
workshops.description: Half-day retreat focused on mindful breathing and gentle movement.

workshops.title: Healthy Weeknight Cooking
workshops.description: Hands-on cooking class focused on fast, nutritious dinners.

workshops.title: Creative Writing Night: Flash Fiction
workshops.description: Generate new story ideas with timed prompts and supportive peer feedback.

workshops.title: Deep Stretch & Relax Yoga Workshop
workshops.description: A special two-hour restorative yoga session with guided relaxation and breathwork.
workshops.prerequisites: Comfortable clothing; mats and props provided.

forum_topics.title: Tuesday/Thursday Pickup Basketball Details
forum_topics.body: Following the recent announcement about new pickup basketball nights, this thread is for anyone who wants to coordinate which nights they plan to attend and what level of play they are looking for. Please share whether you prefer casual half-court or more competitive full-court games, and if you have any suggestions about warm-up drills or basic house rules we should all follow to keep games moving smoothly.

forum_topics.title: Share your beginner 5K training plans
forum_topics.body: I saw that the running group is testing a new 5K evening route along the river and I would love to build up to running the whole loop without walking. For those of you who started as beginners, what weekly schedule worked for you? How many run days versus rest or cross-training days did you use? If you have tips on realistic pacing, good stretches, or playlists that keep you motivated, please post them here so newer runners like me can learn from your experience.

forum_topics.title: Suggestions for future Poetry & Open Mic themes
forum_topics.body: The Poetry & Spoken Word Open Mic has been a highlight for me each month, and Id love to hear ideas for future themed nights. Would people be interested in a "nature and seasons" theme, or maybe a night focused on short humorous pieces? It might also be fun to have a collaborative reading where multiple voices are part of the same poem or story. Please share your ideas and let the organizers know what would encourage you to read or attend more often.

forum_topics.title: Tips for new members joining the club
forum_topics.body: I just signed up for the Evening Saver Combo membership and I am still getting familiar with all the facilities and programs. For members who have been around for a while, what are the top things you wish you had known in your first month here? Are there specific classes that are easier for beginners to drop into, or quieter times in the fitness center or pool? Any advice about locker room etiquette, reserving rooms for small gatherings, or meeting other members would be really appreciated.

forum_topics.title: Rainy day ideas at the club for kids under 8
forum_topics.body: On rainy weekends we like to get out of the house, and the club has been a great option. Besides the Family Music & Movement class and Family Game Night, what other activities have you found that work well for younger kids, especially under age 8? Have you reserved the Kids Activity Room for small playdates, or joined the Family Crafters Circle? Please share what has worked well, including approximate times of day that tend to be less crowded.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Family Spring Fair Prep Day - Setup/Cleanup Crew
volunteer_opportunities.description: Help get the club ready for the Family Spring Fair by setting up tables, hanging decorations, organizing craft areas, and doing light cleanup afterward. Families are welcome to volunteer together, and tasks will be matched to age and ability.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Family Movie Night Ushers & Snack Helpers
volunteer_opportunities.description: Welcome families to our monthly Family Movie Night by handing out programs, helping guests find seats, and assisting at the snack table. Light cleanup after the screening is included.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Community Riverfront Cleanup
volunteer_opportunities.description: Join fellow members to pick up litter along the riverfront trail next to the club. Gloves, bags, and grabbers will be provided. Children may participate with a supervising adult.
volunteer_opportunities.location: Meet in Main Lobby, then Riverfront Trail

volunteer_opportunities.title: Food Drive Sorting & Packing
volunteer_opportunities.description: Sort donated food items by type, check expiration dates, and pack boxes for delivery to the local food pantry. Some light lifting may be required, but there are also seated tasks available.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Teen Board Game Night Hosts
volunteer_opportunities.description: Help run our Teen Board Game Night by teaching rules, matching players to games, and keeping the space tidy. Ideal for volunteers who enjoy games and working with teens.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Senior Luncheon Servers & Setup
volunteer_opportunities.description: Assist with our monthly Senior Luncheon by setting tables, serving meals, refilling beverages, and helping with light cleanup. Friendly conversation with guests is encouraged.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Family Craft Day Activity Helpers
volunteer_opportunities.description: Support our Family Craft Day by restocking supplies, demonstrating simple craft steps, and assisting families at each table. Tasks are designed so that older kids can volunteer alongside a parent or guardian.
volunteer_opportunities.location: Kids Activity Room and Art Studio

volunteer_opportunities.title: Front Desk Greeter - Evening Shift
volunteer_opportunities.description: Greet visitors, answer basic questions, and direct guests to events and rooms. This is a great opportunity for members who enjoy welcoming new people to the club.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Family Fall Fest Activity Stations (Past)
volunteer_opportunities.description: Volunteers ran game booths, craft tables, and snack stands during the annual Family Fall Fest. This listing remains for record-keeping purposes.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Winter Gala Setup & Coat Check (Past)
volunteer_opportunities.description: Volunteers assisted with setting up decorations, managing the coat check, and helping guests during the Winter Gala fundraiser.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Community Health Fair Guides (Cancelled)
volunteer_opportunities.description: This opportunity was originally planned to support our Community Health Fair by guiding visitors between screenings and information tables but has been cancelled.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Club Library Reorganization Day
volunteer_opportunities.description: Help refresh the clubs small lending library by sorting donated books, updating labels, and reorganizing shelves by category. Some tasks are seated and detail-oriented.
